message:
feat(extensions): add HostcallInterceptor trait for deterministic test stubs

Introduce a test-friendly extension point that allows conformance tests
to short-circuit hostcall dispatch with deterministic responses:

HostcallInterceptor trait (src/extensions.rs):
- Define trait with single method: intercept(&self, &HostcallRequest)
  -> Option<HostcallOutcome>
- Return Some(outcome) to bypass real dispatch, None to fall through
- Trait bounds: Send + Sync for thread-safe test fixtures

JsRuntimeHost integration:
- Add optional interceptor: Option<Arc<dyn HostcallInterceptor>> field
- Wire into dispatch_hostcall_allowed() — check interceptor before
  real exec/http/ui handlers
- Refactor JsExtensionRuntimeHandle::start() to delegate to start_inner()
- Add start_with_interceptor() public constructor for test usage

This enables conformance tests to provide canned exec/http/ui responses
without requiring real shell access, network, or terminal, making the
full extension lifecycle testable in CI sandboxes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
